questions.questionText: A patient presents with blindness, but neurological examination and investigations are completely normal. The patient is not faking (not malingering). This is most likely:
questions.optionB: Conversion disorder (functional neurological symptom disorder — motor/sensory symptoms incompatible with neurological disease, associated with psychological stressors, 'la belle indifference')
questions.explanation: Core concept: Conversion disorder = neurological symptoms without organic basis, not intentionally produced. Why correct: Conversion (functional) disorder: motor (paralysis, movement disorders) or sensory (blindness, anesthesia) symptoms incompatible with neurological pathology. Patient is NOT faking (unlike malingering = intentional + external motivation; factitious = intentional + sick role motivation). Classic: 'la belle indifference' (apparent lack of concern). Why A wrong: Factitious disorder (Munchausen): symptoms deliberately produced for sick role (secondary gain = medical attention). Why C wrong: Somatic symptom disorder: physical symptoms WITH excessive anxiety/health preoccupation. Why D wrong: Illness anxiety disorder: preoccupation with having serious disease with minimal or no symptoms. Clinical relevance: Conversion often related to psychological conflict or stressor. Treatment: reassurance, CBT, physical rehabilitation. Exam trap: Conversion = NOT intentional; malingering = intentional for external gain; factitious = intentional for sick role. Memory aid: 'Conversion Converts psychological distress into neurological symptoms.' Reference: Ahuja – Psychosomatic.
questions.conceptTag: Conversion disorder blindness la belle indifference
